seoTitle: How to Use Microsoft Loop Components in Outlook
metaDescription: Embed live, editable Microsoft Loop tables and task lists directly inside Outlook emails so recipients can collaborate without a reply thread.
intro: Long email reply chains are a slow way to get a team's input on a shared table or task list. This guide shows how to insert a live Microsoft Loop component directly into an Outlook email so recipients can edit it inline, and how to keep that same component synced with Microsoft Teams.

steps.heading: Start a new email
steps.body: Open Outlook, click New Email, and set up your recipient list as you normally would.

steps.heading: Insert a Loop component
steps.body: Go to the ribbon toolbar, click the Insert or Message tab, and select the Loop Components icon.

steps.heading: Choose your component type
steps.body: Select a table, task list, or Q&A container from the dropdown based on what kind of collaboration you need.

steps.heading: Add content
steps.body: Give the component a title and type initial notes directly inside the bordered component box.

steps.heading: Sync the component to Teams
steps.body: Click the copy link icon in the upper right corner of the block to paste the same live component into a Teams chat.

steps.heading: Send and track edits
steps.body: Send the email and watch live cursor updates as recipients edit the content inline, without ever needing to start a reply thread.

faq.question: Can recipients edit a Loop component without a Microsoft account?
faq.answer: Recipients generally need appropriate Microsoft 365 access to edit inline, since the component syncs through your organization's cloud environment.

faq.question: Where does a Loop component inserted into an email get saved?
faq.answer: It's automatically saved inside your personal OneDrive for Business, separate from the email itself.

faq.question: Can I control who can edit versus just view the component?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust link permission settings to switch between global editing access and restricted read-only access.

faq.question: Does the component stay live after I send the email?
faq.answer: Yes, it remains an interactive, editable element for recipients even after the email has been sent.
